projects.name: CAPT
projects.subtitle: Governed cognitive architecture
projects.thesis: CAPT stores reasoning as modular, inspectable components. Instead of opaque prompt history, decisions retain their origin and evidence.
projects.description: CAPT is a modular cognitive architecture for persistent, inspectable AI systems. It separates memory, reasoning, and action into governed components.
projects.before: AI remembers you inside someone else's system.

proof.for: Developers building local or sovereign AI

inv.human: Understand how your own AI systems could be governed and inspected.
inv.builder: Core substrate: memory, protocol, orchestration components.

projects.name: bioCAPT
projects.subtitle: Embodied intelligence research
projects.thesis: Biological data remains governed when the body becomes part of the signal — not part of the product.
projects.description: bioCAPT learns from biological state, somatic context, and lived experience. Intelligence is temporal, relational, and embodied — not disembodied text prediction.
projects.before: Intelligence begins and ends with language.
projects.after: Cognition is embodied. The body is part of the signal.

proof.limit: Research stage; not a shipped product

inv.human: See how your body's signals could inform a system that serves you.

projects.name: FrankenCAPT
projects.subtitle: Skill governance
projects.thesis: Capability without governance becomes an uncontrolled pile of tools — not intelligence.
projects.description: FrankenCAPT ingests, evaluates, composes, and governs capabilities. It keeps the ecosystem coherent without bottlenecking growth.
projects.before: More tools automatically mean more intelligence.
projects.after: Capabilities become useful when curated, bounded, and composable.

inv.human: Know that the tools you use are curated, not piled.
inv.researcher: Skill governance as a research problem.

projects.name: SynSync
projects.subtitle: State technology
projects.thesis: Neuroacoustic tools remain understandable and user-directed when sound is designed as an interface — not passive ambience.
projects.description: SynSync builds acoustic protocols for attention, entrainment, and state transition. Safety, agency, and honest claims are foreground constraints.
projects.after: Sound becomes a calibrated, transparent state interface.

proof.limit: Early access; claims are measured, not certified

inv.human: Use a real system for attention and rest today.

projects.name: Knowledge Bubbles
projects.subtitle: Portable knowledge
projects.thesis: Knowledge Bubbles attach source, confidence, and contradiction to every claim. A fact becomes portable instead of a detached sentence.
projects.description: Knowledge Bubbles preserve a claim together with its source, confidence, contradictions, and consent rules. That lets knowledge move between systems without losing the context that made it trustworthy.
projects.before: A fact is a detached sentence.
projects.after: Knowledge carries context, provenance, confidence, and disagreement.

inv.human: Your notes could carry their own sources and doubts.
inv.researcher: Provenance as a first-class data property.

projects.name: CTP
projects.subtitle: Transaction protocol
projects.thesis: CTP wraps each agent request in an explicit contract. Instead of opaque message passing, work travels with its evidence and accountability.
projects.description: CTP structures cognitive requests, responses, evidence, and state transitions so agents can collaborate without dissolving accountability.
projects.before: Agents pass opaque messages and hope.
projects.after: Cognitive work travels through explicit, inspectable transactions.

inv.human: Know that AI-to-AI deals are visible, not hidden.

projects.name: KHSB
projects.subtitle: System nervous system
projects.thesis: KHSB connects local systems through bounded envelopes. Instead of open borders, interconnection uses explicit contracts and identity.
projects.description: KHSB is the communication tissue connecting systems through bounded envelopes, routing, identity, and protocol-aware exchange.
projects.before: Connectivity means opening every system to every other system.
projects.after: Systems communicate through explicit boundaries and contracts.

inv.human: Your devices talk to each other on your terms.

projects.name: Human Metadata Engine
projects.subtitle: Interpretive synthesis
projects.thesis: A person cannot be reduced to one score or model. Multiple interpretive lenses can coexist without pretending to be absolute truth.
projects.description: Synthesizes multiple lenses — biological, behavioral, symbolic — while preserving uncertainty, source boundaries, and agency.
projects.before: One framework explains the whole person.
projects.after: Multiple lenses coexist without pretending to be absolute truth.

inv.human: See yourself through more than one lens, honestly.

projects.name: Inversion Excursion
projects.subtitle: Field discovery
projects.thesis: The overlooked world is full of signal. Observation deserves a place before certainty.
projects.description: Carries the lab into ecology, mycology, medicine, place, and direct observation — where knowledge is encountered before it is abstracted.
projects.before: Knowledge only becomes real after institutions certify it.
projects.after: Direct observation begins inquiry. Evidence determines the claim.

inv.human: Join observation walks and field practice.

projects.name: Research
projects.subtitle: Evidence layer
projects.thesis: Frontier work becomes credible when its uncertainty is visible — not when its claims are loudest.
projects.description: Preserves architecture notes, experiments, benchmarks, limitations, failures, and the boundary between what is observed and what is merely hoped.
projects.after: Claims earn trust through evidence, provenance, and reproducibility.

inv.human: Read what we actually found, including failures.
inv.researcher: Evidence layer for the whole lab.

projects.name: Field Notes
projects.subtitle: Observation practice
projects.thesis: Observation labeled honestly is valuable even before certainty arrives.
projects.description: Preserves ecological, medicinal, technical, and experiential observations with their source type and uncertainty intact.
projects.after: Observation can be valuable when labeled honestly and tested carefully.

projects.name: Repositories
projects.subtitle: Working evidence
projects.thesis: Code is not proof of a vision — but it is evidence the vision is being built.
projects.description: Exposes implementation, architecture, release state, tests, and the gap between concept and production candidate.
projects.before: A polished claim proves a system exists.
projects.after: Artifacts, tests, and reproducible builds establish what is real.

inv.human: See the actual code behind the claims.

projects.name: Documentation
projects.subtitle: Architecture memory
projects.thesis: Systems become governable when their decisions remain legible — not when the documentation was written once and forgotten.
projects.description: Preserves the why behind interfaces, protocols, safety boundaries, and release decisions so the architecture can evolve without forgetting itself.
projects.before: Documentation describes software after the fact.
projects.after: Documentation is part of the system's memory and governance.
